paragraphs.body: Where I am from An Essay on identity By  Laguna Beach A small coastal city in Orange County, California. Known as a seaside resort city, Laguna has scenic coves, beaches, and many art galleries. Walking down the concrete path, my feet slide forward in my flip-flops strangling my toes. Relief is found at the bottom, they wriggle in the hot sand, like every other summer as far back as I can remember. Laguna Beach is a premier coastal destination for locals and tourists alike.

annotations.text: Walking down the concrete path, my feet slide forward in my flip-flops strangling my toes. Relief is found at the bottom, they wriggle in the hot sand, like every other summer as far back as I can remember.

paragraphs.body: Pine Creek Gorge Sometimes called the Grand Canyon of Pennsylvania, this 47-mile gorge carves through the Allegheny Plateau with steep forested canyons, waterfalls, and lookouts. The river shines brilliantly far below in between walls of lush foliage. I am bored now, luckily home is just a short drive away. The PA Grand Canyon offers hiking and biking on the Pine Creek Rail Trail as well as wagon ride tours through the canyon. The word kebab is also a Persian word and appears in the poetry of the Persian Rudaki, in the 9th century AD. Therefore, any country that serves this dish under the name Kebab has been influenced by kebab from Iran. Sliding off the seekh, it lay upon a bed of rice with grilled vegetables surrounding, sumac sprinkled from above, this must be as good as good gets. With a simple list of ingredients including lamb or beef, white or yellow onion, salt, and black pepper and the proper technique when grilling, one can taste the reason this dish is so popular.

Boeing 737-800 Launched on September 5, 1994, and its first flight on July 31, 1997, this aircraft could fly up to 189 passengers up to 3,115 nautical miles. The 737-800 is the most widely used narrowbody aircraft and competes primarily with the Airbus A320. The plane shuddered and suddenly dropped in altitude enough to make passengers scream in terror. I wasn’t scared, because I knew that turbulence presents no threat to the aircraft. The greater threat would be panic if anything. The 737-800 burns 850 US gallons (3,200 L) of Jet fuel per hour. The 737-800 was the first US aircraft to operate via a blend of algae-derived biofuel and traditional jet fuel to reduce its carbon footprint.

Cedar Run A small village nestled in a valley along Pine Creek in Lycoming County, Pennsylvania. Cedar Run’s origins lie in the early logging industry and the abundance of fish and game were a powerful draw for early settlers of this area. My grandmother’s old cottage was right next to the river, so close I could cautiously climb down the rocky path to come down to the river to play. Then the general store for ice cream. The unincorporated community is home to the Cedar Run General Store and Cedar Run Inn, both of which opened in the 1890s and remain open to this day. The last train passed through in 1989 before the track were removed and the Rails to Trails project implemented in the area.

Laurel Festival Every year in mid-June the community of Wellsboro celebrates The blooming of the state flower, the Mountain Laurel. Plans for the 83rd Pennsylvania State Laurel Festival are already underway thanks to the committee of volunteers who work tirelessly to ensure its success. Green, fractured Black Walnuts litter the sidewalk. It smells tangy almost like Citrus, the air is comfortable like a blanket. Up ahead the Vendors hand out steaming sticky buns and kielbasa to the masses. The week-long event includes musical concerts, parades, and around 70 vendors every year.

annotations.comment: This section is fascinating, but it is a moment when I would love to see you in the place.

paragraphs.body: Thousand Steps Beach Not truly a thousand steps, more like 218, but it does feel like it. This stunning beach is bordered by Sandstone Cliffs to the North and South. The steps are a filter, only those who are worthy of the trek can be a part of this precious place. The waves here are big I like that a lot, because it is more difficult and more exciting. If You head south along the shoreline you can find laguna’s most Famous sea cave. It has full walkthrough but if the tide comes up it can turn deadly. This location is the best beach of Laguna.

annotations.comment: Hello, What a pleasure it is reading the details about the places you identify as home and self. The central focusing idea of your writing here is the importance of place and space—the landscape of your travels and the sensuous geographies clearly build a sense of self for you and you are sharing that sense of self with your reader. The big ideas present in this piece are seeing, feeling, smelling, tasting, and hearing the world. One of the things that I admire most about this piece is the clarity and intensity with which you write about place. The words that stand out the most to me in this piece are the tactile words of the landscape. You do a great job of showing your reader the sand and the waves, the “steep forested canyons, waterfalls, and lookouts.” By doing this, you make each place distinct and important to your personal identity. I feel a definite sense of presence in your descriptions—you are not merely storing your body in these places, but experiencing them, and you’re sharing those experiences with your reader with clarity and candor. While the writing is certainly lovely, I’m having a hard time determining the audience for this piece. It is a pleasure to read, but I would love to see more interaction between you and the landscape. Currently, the piece often reads like something more of a collage of journal entries, which, lovely as they are, ultimately doesn’t build the essay that could exist here. There are moments like when your feet slip from the flip flops and ground you in the sand, which allow your reader to build the sense of you in these places, but there are also moments where you mention your boredom without really bringing us into that boredom. Because of this, the purpose of the writing seems to be more a series of reflections, but, with a little more work, its purpose could become an invitation to your reader to gain a stronger sense of how these places have created and continue to create the person you are today. One of my favorite details of any piece of art—literature, cinema, sculpture, you name it—is when the artist engages the landscape as more than just setting, but as something of an experience. What’s even better is when the landscape becomes a character as important as any individual could be. You are well on your way to creating just such a document. I’m looking forward to seeing how you continue to develop the connections between the character of your physical world and your place in it. Thanks,
